subject: English
chapter: Journey to the End of the Earth

remember: Recall the plot and characters of the short stories in the chapter
Identify the themes and motifs in the short stories
List the literary devices used by J.G. Farrell in the chapter
understand: Explain the historical context in which the short stories are set
Analyze the role of imperialism in the short stories
Describe the relationship between the colonizers and the colonized in the stories
apply: Compare and contrast the themes and characters of different short stories
Use literary devices to analyze and interpret the texts
Apply historical context to understand the short stories
analyze: Evaluate the impact of imperialism on the people and culture of a particular region
Analyze the use of language and tone in the short stories
Compare the portrayal of imperialism in different short stories

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To understand the historical context of the short stories and its significance in the narrative.
As per NCERT objective 2: To analyze the role of imperialism in shaping the characters and plot of the short stories.
board_exam_objectives: To analyze the themes, characters, and plot of the short stories and their relevance to the chapter's title, 'Journey to the End of the Earth'.
To evaluate the impact of imperialism on the people and culture of a particular region, as depicted in the short stories.
To compare and contrast the themes and characters of different short stories and their historical context.
